round1: Round 1 was a bloodbath for anyone who dared to pick a favorite, turning most brackets into a dumpster fire, though the majority at least got Boston right. Jaclyn and Robin, with their 5 correct picks, are the only ones who can hold their heads vaguely high, while Derrick and Jamie are now officially on the hook for next year's snacks, thanks to their stunning 2-pick performance.
round2: Round 2 proved brutal for many, with Columbus and the Islanders sending more than a few picks straight to the waiver wire. Nathan actually watched hockey, nailing both the Sharks and Bruins series. Meanwhile, Nickall delivered a masterclass in how to pick the losing side in every single matchup.
round3: Round 3 was a perfectly split pool on both series winners, meaning exactly half of you are already out of contention for this round. Nathan correctly picked the Blues in 6 games, while everyone else who picked Boston clearly expected the Hurricanes to put up more of a fight than a four-game sweep. As for Kiersten, you picked Carolina in 4 games – impressive foresight on the series length, just not the winner.
round4: St. Louis took the Cup in 7, a result that means nine of you are now officially wrong. As for the rest, you at least picked the right team, but apparently, nobody knows how to count to seven.
overall: Alright, the 2019 NHL playoffs are done, and frankly, the pool performance was about as exciting as a defensive trap. We've got a four-way tie for "championship" with a measly 5 points, shared by Nathan, adding another trophy to his overflowing cabinet, Jaclyn, finally snapping a nine-year drought, and first-time "champions" Jake and Benedict. Meanwhile, last year's victor Ryan and runner-up Glenda pulled off an astounding dive, joining Marc, Kiersten, and Theodore in the ignominious dead-last club with a big fat zero points, proving that even former champs like Marc can hit rock bottom. It seems picking the Blues to win the Cup was about as common as finding a unicorn, making this one of the most embarrassingly low-scoring pools in recent memory. And for Nickall, well, another year, another 4 points, and still no championship in sight after 17 attempts.
